---
title: "Shauna Heron"
subtitle: Data scientist · Experimental psychology · Human–robot interaction
description-meta: "Shauna Heron — graduate researcher and data scientist working on applied machine learning, clinical analytics, and human–robot interaction."
image: "files/images/me-casual.png"
page-navigation: false
toc: false
#sidebar: false
page-layout: full

about:
  id: hero-heading
  template: trestles
  image-width: 70%
  links:
    - icon: robot
      text: Lab
      href: https://www.github.com
      target: _blank
    - text: "{{< ai arxiv >}} arXiv"
      href: https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Heron%2C+S
      target: _blank
    - icon: envelope
      text: E-mail
      href: mailto:sheron@laurentian.ca   
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://linkedin.com/in/shauna-heron-1885247
      target: _blank
      #target: _blank 
    - text: "{{< ai orcid >}} ORCID"   # use academic icons, "" are needed, you also need to install the fontawesome extension
      href: https://orcid.org/0000-0002-9262-6718     
      target: _blank


listing:   # Comment the listing section and delete corresponding lines in the index.qmd file to remove the recent posts
  - id: recent-posts
    contents: 
      - posts
     # - posts/post-links.yml
    exclude:
      title: ""
    type: grid
    max-items: 3
    image-height: 150px
    page-size: 3 #set same as max-times to remove the page number setting
    fields: [image, date, title, subtitle]
    max-description-length: 75
    sort: 
      - "date desc"
      - "title asc"
---

:::{#hero-heading}

## Hey, I'm Shauna.

Thanks for stopping by!

During the day I work as the data scientist for a community child and youth mental health agency, where I build the models, dashboards, and reproducible reports the clinical teams use. Most of my days involve writing R (and increasingly Python), wrangling big, messy EHR datasets, fitting statistical models to that data, and thinking about how machine-learning systems behave in the real world. It's this work that helps keep me honest about what data can and can't do.

I'm also a researcher finishing up a graduate degree in experimental psychology with a strong computational focus. My research sits in two places that turn out to have more in common than they look: **applied machine learning** on electronic health record data, and **autonomous human–robot interaction**. Both are really questions about what it takes to make a probabilistic system both useful and reliable in a setting where people depend on it.



:::
